note: Bootstrap Layouts registers core Layout plugins (rows/columns) via YAML, adds a shared config form for wrappers/classes/attributes, defines two plugin types (handler + update), and ships no admin UI or config. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which layout system does Bootstrap Layouts plug into, and roughly what layouts does it add? Name the category they appear under and a couple of the layout ids.
evals.expected_output: It registers core Layout Discovery plugins under the 'Bootstrap' category (bs_1col, bs_2col, bs_3col, bs_4col plus _stacked and _bricked variants, 11 total), so they show up in Layout Builder, Display Suite, and Panels.

evals.prompt: How do I use a Bootstrap Layouts grid in Layout Builder or Display Suite on Drupal 11, and does the module have an admin settings page to configure first?
evals.expected_output: There is no admin UI or config route; the module just needs to be installed. You pick a Bootstrap layout when adding a Layout Builder section or a Display Suite layout; settings are stored per display.

evals.prompt: In a Bootstrap Layouts region, how do I set the column width and breakpoint (e.g. make a region span 8 of 12 columns on medium screens)? Where do the class options come from?
evals.expected_output: Pick a class like col-md-8 from the region's Classes multi-select (options col-{xs,sm,md,lg}-{1..12} plus hidden/visible/background/text/utility groups), generated by BootstrapLayoutsManager::getClassOptions().

evals.prompt: What is the single Drupal core module Bootstrap Layouts depends on, and what theme requirement does it have to actually render correctly?
evals.expected_output: It depends on core layout_discovery (Layout Discovery). It needs a Bootstrap-based theme (or one providing .row/.col-* grid classes) to style the output.

evals.prompt: How do I define my own Bootstrap grid layout in a custom module so it reuses Bootstrap Layouts' region config form? Which class and which YAML file?
evals.expected_output: Add an entry to your module's {module}.layouts.yml with class '\Drupal\bootstrap_layouts\Plugin\Layout\BootstrapLayoutsBase', category 'Bootstrap', a template and regions; no PHP needed.

evals.prompt: I want to add my own theme's utility classes to the list of classes selectable in Bootstrap Layouts regions. Which hook do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_bootstrap_layouts_class_options_alter(&$classes, &$groups) to add a group and/or classes to the selectable options.

evals.prompt: Bootstrap Layouts defines its own plugin type for loading/saving layout instances in different consumers (Display Suite, Panels). What is the annotation/handler plugin type and which two handlers ship with it?
evals.expected_output: The BootstrapLayoutsHandler plugin type (manager plugin.manager.bootstrap_layouts). Built-in handlers: 'ds' (Display Suite) and 'page_manager' (Panels/Page Manager).

evals.prompt: In a Bootstrap Layouts layout, how do I change a region's wrapper HTML element and add a custom attribute like an id? What auto-added classes mark each region?
evals.expected_output: Choose the Wrapper element (div, span, section, article, header, footer, aside, figure) and enter attributes as key|value pairs (e.g. id|hero). With 'Add region specific classes' on, each region gets bs-region and bs-region--{name}.
